text.title: portfolio-strategy Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for portfolio-strategy as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Horizon labels becoming political: every team lobbies to be H3 for more freedom and fewer KPIs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use the classification rubric with a neutral arbiter (portfolio-classifier agent) and require revenue_evidence for any H1 label. When classification is evidence-based, lobbying loses its lever.

failure-mode.description: H3 budget starved first when H1 slips: teams then claim they "do innovation" while the budget shows zero.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Lock H3 budget at the start of the quarter before sprint planning. Once H1 work claims all capacity, H3 is invisible in retrospect. Pre-commitment is the protection.

failure-mode.description: Treating 70/20/10 as empirically optimal rather than a starting heuristic.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The numbers originated as a Google PR artifact from 2005. Treat them as a reasonable starting point, calibrate to your market and stage, and document why you deviated if you do.

failure-mode.description: Three Horizons assumes time-sequenced horizons (H3 becomes H2 becomes H1). Many SaaS bets never graduate; they die or stay niche.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pure time framing misleads. Instruct the agent that horizons are about risk + evidence today, not a fixed escalator.

failure-mode.description: Cross-portfolio dependencies (shared platform, shared auth, shared data pipeline) are invisible in horizon math.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Surface cross-portfolio dependencies in a separate matrix; allocation math alone hides them.

failure-mode.description: Classifier over-tagging items as H2/H3 because the prose description sounds novel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force requirement: revenue_evidence field must be present and non-empty for H1 classification. Default all items without evidence to H3 with low confidence. Novelty of language is not a classification criterion.

failure-mode.description: Agent computing allocation percentages from ticket counts instead of engineering cost.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validate the input schema before classifying. If eng_cost_usd or person_weeks is missing, refuse to compute allocation percentages and return an error prompting for the field.

failure-mode.description: Macro condition buried in a long prompt and silently dropped by the rebalancer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pass macro condition as a structured field in a dedicated XML element, not as a sentence in prose. The model must output the condition it used in the JSON response for auditability.

failure-mode.description: Rebalancer hallucinating new initiatives to fill H3 budget ("suggest H3 bets").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Constrain the rebalancer to picking from the existing list. If new H3 bets are needed, run a separate brainstorm cycle and feed the survivors back in as classified items.

failure-mode.description: Agent making the rebalance decision unsupervised when drift exceeds 3pp.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The allocation decision is a CPO call, not an agent call. Automate drift detection and alerting; escalate to a human when any horizon drifts beyond the threshold. Never let the agent commit a rebalance to production.
